data.title: Volvo Results Unlikely to Change Earnings Expectations — Market Talk
data.description: Volvo's first-quarter update probably won't move consensus expectations for earnings much, Bernstein analysts write. The company delivered a 4% EBIT beat as a strong service business and cost control drove margins above consensus, Bernstein says. It is also positive that the company sees no demand impact as yet from the Middle East conflict and high fuel prices. Truck orders beat consensus by 9%, driven by North America, although this didn't lead to an upgrade of market forecasts in the region. Heavy and medium duty truck order intake in Europe was down and the tariff impact will worsen again in the second quarter, Bernstein adds. "We expect a small positive reaction today, with debate to focus on conservatism ('why did you not increase the market outlook for North America?') rather than any negative surprise." (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Japan's Nikkei closes at record high as tech earnings overshadow Mideast concerns
data.description:
By Rocky Swift

Japan's Nikkei set a closing record high on Friday, capping a third consecutive weekly gain, as enthusiasm over technology sector earnings offset uncertainty over a potential peace deal in the Middle East.

The benchmark Nikkei 225 Index rose 0.97% to close at an unprecedented 59,716.18. The gauge rose 2.1% on the week. The broader Topix eked out a 0.01% advance to end at 3,716.59.

The Nikkei briefly broke through the psychologically key 60,000 mark for the first time on Thursday, more than recouping all its losses since the war broke out in Iran almost two months ago and spread around the region.

Lebanon and Israel extended their ceasefire for three weeks after a high-level meeting at the White House, U.S. President Donald Trump said on Thursday. Trump also said the U.S. would not use a nuclear weapon against Iran, following the extension of a ceasefire with Tehran earlier in the week.

Overnight, chipmaker Intel forecast second-quarter revenue that beat Wall Street expectations as demand surges for the company's server processors used for artificial intelligence in data centres. Ibiden , a Japanese supplier to Intel, soared 12.6% in Tokyo, leading gains on the Nikkei.

"Today's Japanese stock market is being driven upward by a sense of relief regarding the situation in the Middle East, along with continued expectations of earnings growth from the expanding AI sector," said Wataru Akiyama, an equities strategist at Nomura Securities.

There were 92 advancers on the Nikkei index against 131 decliners. After Ibiden, the steepest gainers were Denka , up 8.3%, and chip industry supplier Advantest , which advanced 5.5%.

Canon was among the steepest decliners on the Nikkei, losing 7.9%. After the bell on Thursday, the company announced a downward revision to its earnings forecast.

data.title: French consumer confidence falls sharply in April as war takes toll on economy
data.description:
French consumer confidence fell sharply in April, recording its largest drop since the start of the Ukraine war in 2022, the INSEE official statistics agency said on Friday.

Economies worldwide are feeling the inflationary impact of the Iran war since attacks on energy infrastructure in the Middle East and Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz have led to the largest oil supply disruption in years.

The consumer confidence index dropped in April to 84 points, from 89 points in March, INSEE data showed. That was far below the 88 points expected on average by 15 economists polled by Reuters.

"In April 2026, households' views on their personal financial situation, both past and future, deteriorated significantly," the statistics office said.

Concerns over unemployment also increased, it added, with the corresponding index gaining 7 points to remain well above its long-term average.

Households' perception of past inflation surged. The share of consumers reporting sharp price increases over the past year jumped 30 points, the largest monthly increase in more than 40 years, INSEE said.

Private sector growth in the euro zone nearly stalled in March, the first full month since the beginning of the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran. France's business confidence also weakened markedly, as companies cited risks to demand and inflation from the conflict.

data.title: UK Stocks-Factors to watch on April 24
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen opening lower on Friday, with futures down 0.8%.

* MONDI: Mondi said the company is responding to increased costs from the Iran war by raising prices, and that it expects the impact of some of those moves to fully reflect in the third quarter.

* COMPUTACENTER: Computacenter said it expects to deliver full-year results comfortably ahead of market expectations after a strong quarter driven by booming demand from data centres and AI projects in North America and the UK.

* JD SPORTS: JD Sports chair Andy Higginson quit after pushing for chief executive Regis Schultz to be ousted and failing to win unanimous backing for the move from the retailer’s board, the Financial Times reported.

* TARIFFS: U.S. President Donald Trump said the United States will retaliate with a "big tariff" if Britain continues to target companies like Apple , Alphabet's Google and Meta with its technology tax.

* RETAIL SALES: British motorists rushed to buy fuel immediately after the start of the Iran war, pushing up total sales volumes for the country's retail sector by more than expected in March, official data showed on Friday.

* COMMODITIES: Oil prices rose on Friday, while gold prices edged lower, and copper slipped.

* For more on the factors affecting European stocks, please click on:

data.title: Nomura posts slim Q4 profit rise but annual earnings hit record
data.description:
By Anton Bridge

Japan's largest investment bank and brokerage Nomura reported a 3% rise in fourth-quarter net profit on Friday, as wealth management-related revenues rose and its wholesale division capitalised on buoyant markets and dealmaking.

Nomura's net income rose to 73.9 billion yen ($462.60 million) for the January-March quarter, up from 72 billion yen over the same period a year earlier.

It generated its highest ever annual income of 362.1 billion yen, beating the previous record of 340.7 billion last year.

The record results show the fruits of Nomura's multi-year effort to build a business model centred on stable fee-based revenues that are less exposed to oscillating market conditions.

Nomura has developed a dominant position in Japan's wealth management market with a strong base of recurring fee revenues. It also benefitted from the flow fees generated by market volatility over the quarter.

The wholesale division, which houses Nomura's investment banking and trading arms, booked its highest annual revenue since the division was established in April 2010.

($1 = 159.7500 yen)

data.title: Yara Q1 Net Income Rises
data.description:
OSLO (dpa-AFX) - Yara (YAR.OL) reported that its first quarter net income attributable to shareholders of the parent increased to $326 million from $294 million, prior year. Basic earnings per share was $1.28 compared to $1.15. EBITDA excluding special items was $896 million compared to $638 million. Adjusted earnings per share excluding foreign currency exchange gain/loss and special items was $1.64 compared to $1.01.

First quarter revenue and other income was $4.26 billion compared to $3.65 billion, prior year. Total deliveries were 2% higher than for the same quarter a year ago.

data.title: Bitcoin Falls as Iran War Uncertainty Keeps Investors Cautious — Market Talk
data.description: Bitcoin falls as investors remain cautious on risky assets after President Trump announced a three-week extension to the cease-fire with Iran but suggested he wouldn't rush a long-term peace deal. Trump also ordered the U.S. military to "shoot and kill" Iranian boats that deploy mines in the critical Strait of Hormuz. "Markets are entering the final day of the trading week in a cautious mood as U.S.-Iran tensions show no signs of easing while the Strait of Hormuz remains essentially closed," Deutsche Bank analysts say in a note. Bitcoin falls 0.1% to $77,837, LSEG data show.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Asahi Group's Japan Operations Under Pressure, Marketing Costs Set to Rise — Market Talk
data.description: Asahi Group's Japan operations are under pressure, and its marketing costs are poised to increase, Morningstar's Jacky Tsang says in a research report. The Japanese company's domestic beer sales fell by more than 15% on year in 1Q and were below Morningstar's expectations. Asahi Group's selling, general and administrative costs ratio is likely to remain above 28% in 2026 as it invests in key markets to drive sales recovery, the analyst says. Its expenses related to cybersecurity investments and digital transformation are also likely to increase. Morningstar lowers its 2026 earnings forecast for Asahi by 7% and trims the stock's fair-value estimate by 3% to 2,160.0 yen. Shares close 0.6% lower at 1,558.0 yen. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Associated Banc-Corp reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Associated Banc-Corp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 70 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 63 cents. The mean expectation of ten analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 68 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 63 cents to 71 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 10% to $379.35 million from a year ago; analysts expected $384.18 million.

  • Associated Banc-Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was 70 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $116.76 million.

  • Associated Banc-Corp shares had risen by 7.9% this quarter and gained 8.3% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 1.2% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 4 "strong buy" or "buy," 6 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the banks peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Associated Banc-Corp is $30.00, about 7% above its last closing price of $27.91

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 24 at 06:51 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: South Korean shares post third straight weekly rise
data.description:
Key points:
  • KOSPI flat, foreigners net sellers
  • Korean won weakens against dollar
  • South Korea benchmark bond yield rises
  • For the midday report, please clicknL1N41701R

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares posted their third straight weekly rise on Friday, even as the market closed flat for the day as losses in auto and chip makers offset gains in battery makers.

** The benchmark KOSPI closed at 6,475.63, down 0.18 points, or less than 0.01%, from a record close of 6,575.81 on Thursday.

** The KOSPI rose 4.6% this week, bringing its year-to-date gains to 54%. In 2025, the index rose 76%, the biggest since 1999.

** Battery maker LG Energy Solution climbed 3.11% and peer Samsung SDI rose 1.75%, extending a recent rally.

** Chipmaker Samsung Electronics fell 2.23% and peer SK Hynix lost 0.24%, after posting record closes in the last session on upbeat data such as earnings and gross domestic product.

** Hyundai Motor and sister automaker Kia Corp were down 3.57% and down 3.16%, respectively.

** "Foreign selling was concentrated in the chip sector, as short-term over-valuations compared with earnings triggered profit-taking sentiment," said Lee Kyoung-min, an analyst at Daishin Securities.

** Foreigners were net sellers of shares worth 1.9 trillion won ($1.28 billion).

** Of the total 898 traded issues, 622 shares advanced, while 237 declined.

** The won was quoted at 1,484.5 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.32% lower than its previous close at 1,479.7.

** In money and debt markets, June futures on three-year treasury bonds (KTBc1) lost 0.14 point to 103.84.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) rose by 3.7 basis points to 3.495%, while the benchmark 10-year yield rose by 2.6 basis points to 3.815%.

($1 = 1,483.1600 won)
